entry.title: Re: Creation of accounts
entry.content:
Hello,

create g/l accounts - FS00

posting keys - OB41

customer & vendor - BP or FD01+FK01.

Have nice day.

V.

entry.title: CDS view error Unexpected word er zxxx_c_rules
entry.content:
SAP Experts, 

created cds view with naming convention zxxx_c_rules in higher version S4 2022

and transported to lower version S4 1809 

Transport ended with error, its unable to create CDS view 'define root view entity' in 1809

Data Definition : Zxxx_c_rules

Unexpected word "Zxxx_c_rules"

i can identify this relevant note , but there's no correction instruction in this note. is there any supporting note related to this ?

Thanks

entry.title: CDS negative FLTP cast to decimal or floating in 7.5
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am using CDS to extract material classification data, transpose it to columns and expose to odata. It went well untill I discoverend that we have negative decimal values in the classification fields. Those values get zeroed in the cds conversion. 

I created 2 cds views.

1 cds. Selects material classification data.

Select OBJEK, ATFLV from AUSP

2  cds. Transposes classification row data to columns using function MAX. 

max ( case cl_name when 'TOTAL' then

case when alt_value <> 0 then alt_value else 0

end else 0

end ) as total.

I used CDS function MAX to return decimals but it does not convert NEGATIVE floating quantity fields  AUSP-ATFLV = -0,0018 (-1.8000000000000000E-03).

MAX returns 0 if the value is negative. I am on version NW 7.5 but even the newer  FLTP_TO_DEC cannot use negative literal.

Function AVG returns floating type values but I do not need average calculation. 

Can anyone  help to find a solution?

Regards,

Egle

entry.title: Re: Take SAP Certification Exams in Your Own Language
entry.content:
Very helpful. Unfortunately, many people don't realise this.

Thank you very much, Suzanne!

entry.title: Re: SAP EBS: Question on Interpretation Algorithm "20 020...
entry.content: Hi Anita, thank you for such detailed reply! I did not know about the Processing Rules. We will be switching to SAP S/4HANA (Private cloud or on premise - I am not sure) in the next couple of years and I will take this opportunity to convert all our posting rules to processing rules. I have saved your links for the future reference 🙂 For now, I will stick with posting rules in this client because of the time constraints. Are you able to confirm, if the posting rule algorithm 20 020 is able to find open documents to be cleared on another clearing account, than the one used for bank posting?

entry.title: Re: Table Input Format for Anomaly Detection in HANA_ML
entry.content:
I can reprodue the issue with hana-ml version: 2.23.240121700. INTEGER is accepted for column ID, but BIGINT isn't:

ERROR:hana_ml.algorithms.pal.preprocessing:(423, 'AFL error: AFL DESCRIBE for nested call failed - invalid table(s) for ANY-procedure call (Input table 0: column 0 has invalid SQL type.): line 10 col 1 (at pos 399)')

entry.title: SAP Training in Germany
entry.content:
Hi, 

I am a Data Engineer consultant and I have been studying/working for the past year SAP Datasphere and integrations of non-SAP sources and SAP sources. However, I am struggling to understanding all integrations and complexity layers from SAP products. 

Does anyone knows a SAP training in English on site in Germany focusing in Data Services Data Integration mixed with ERP systems? Or any speciallist that would be interest on giving a training? 

Thank you in advance. 

#training #community #sapdatasphere

entry.title: Re: SD Pricing
entry.content: Have you checked your pricing procedure for this condition type what steps are set to calculate the value ?

entry.title: Re: SAP EBS: Question on Interpretation Algorithm "20 020: Document Number Search"
entry.content: @joerg_rolapp thank you for your comment! Just to make sure we are on the same page: in one client where this solution (posting rule with algorithm 20 020) is working fine uses 1 clearing account for both posting payment documents (DR vendor CR cl. acc.) and outgoing payments on bank statement (DR cl. acc CR bank). My question was about a case where 2 different clearing accounts are used and algorithm fails to do the clearing. My suspicion was that it just does not "see" the open payment document (DR vendor CR cl. acc. 1) when posting an outgoing bank payment (DR cl. acc. 2 CR bank). A manual posting is possible witohut issues (DR vendor CR cl. acc. 1 --- DR cl. acc. 2 CR bank --- DR cl. acc. 1 CR cl. acc. 2)

entry.title: Re: how to create sales order for available stock of mate...
entry.content: Not possible because, the MRP settings in the master data and Requirement Type in Sales Order will be different.  What is the Business requirement to meet such non-standard functionality ?

Hi,

You may need use the valuation type for different material utilization purposes.

Please refer to the following blog for Split Valuation in Inventory Management,

Split Valuation in Inventory Management on SAP S/4... - SAP Community

Hope it helps.

Kind Regards,

Lucy

entry.title: Re: SD Pricing
entry.content:
Hello sir,

Is it because it is adding up the value of the ZRFP and ZAPR for the manual discount due to the steps in the pricing procedure?

Why does the condition basis take the rebate factor if this condition is inactive in the proforma invoice?

entry.title: Enable HTTPS port on SAP JAVA - SSL port not active
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I need to enable the HTTPS port on a SAP JAVA server. 

I am getting: "SSL port not active" after activating using the SSL Wizard as per SAP note.

Is it required to purcase a CSR response from a CA to enable the HTTPS port?   (This is a DEV system -> was hoping not to request a paid cert for the DEV system).

Other checks done -> "sapcrypto.dl" is already active in the EXE directory after install and parameters service/protectedwebmethods and jstartup/service_acl addes as per SAP note 

2510099.

 

Please assist.

 

Kind Regards,

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Implementation: Module Sequence and Logic
entry.content:
Hmmm...that’s an impressively broad question, almost like asking how long it takes to build a 'house' without specifying its size, bedrooms, layout, design, or materials.

The duration of a SuccessFactors implementation, quite obviously, depends on multiple factors, including Scope & Modules, Company Size & HR process Complexity, Data Migration, Customizations, Integrations, Stakeholder Involvement, Change Management & Training, Regulatory & Compliance Requirements, Implementation Approach, Client & Partner Efficiency, Testing & UAT, and the Countries & Regions being deployed in. If there’s a specific scenario you’re referring to, sharing more details would make for a more meaningful discussion.

entry.title: Creating/enabling a new WFA on HANA instance
entry.content:
Hello @YSE-1 

We would like to enable WFA on HANA for a new client and this is our first implementation of WFA. We followed the instructions from the Implementation Kit and raised a support case with SAP to check provisioning settings and upload the standard measure template, however their response was the following: 

Could you please let us know whether the provisioning settings and enablement of standard measures is still done by SAP or do we need to do this as the implementation partner?

On our side, we already ticked the "Customer has Workforce Analytics License" setting in provisioning. What would be the next steps in order to create the WFA instance? Are there any additional steps that we have to follow to enable/create the WFA on HANA instance or is it done by SAP?

Many thanks in advance for your answers.

Best regards,

Marina

entry.title: Re: EMPLOYEE GROUPING 1DOES NOT EXIST (PPNGL feature contains errors)
entry.content: Hello Team,we have deactivated FK field (Field mom).Still we have that error.also thre sysmbolic account is not used by us.no wage type is assigned.but why this issue.do we have to run any program or .please advise.

entry.title: Re: Update procedure for SAP Public Cloud version and too...
entry.content:
Hello,

To ask the first question, the installation of a release in SAP S/4HANA Cloud follows a standardized process managed by SAP since it is a Cloud SaaS (Software as a Service) solution. Here are the main steps:

 

1. Planning and Communication
SAP publishes a release schedule with new updates (usually two major updates per year).
SAP informs customers of the dates through the SAP Roadmap Explorer and the SAP Maintenance Planner.
Notifications are sent via SAP for Me and SAP Cloud ALM.
 
2. Preparation of the Environment
Testing in the pre-production system: SAP first deploys the new version in a test system (Quality Tenant) about four weeks before going live.
Customers and partners can test new processes and validate specific adaptations (customizations, Fiori extensions, integrations).
 
3. Automated Deployment by SAP
SAP automatically executes the update on the production environment on the scheduled date.
The installation includes:
Updating the S/4HANA core
Applying patches and fixes
Activating new features
 
The process is carried out with minimal downtime to ensure business continuity.
 
4. Post-Installation Validation
SAP runs automated tests to ensure the system is working properly.
Users must perform business process tests to validate critical operations.
 
 
To ask the second question: yes, SAP offers several tools to test and validate the side effects of an SAP S/4HANA Cloud release. Here are the main ones:
 
1. SAP Cloud ALM (Application Lifecycle Management)
The central tool for cloud application lifecycle management.
Helps track changes, test new features, and manage the impact of updates.
Integrates automated testing and dashboards to identify version discrepancies.
 
2. SAP Test Automation Tool
Executes automated regression tests after an update.
SAP provides pre-configured test scenarios to validate core business processes.
Accessible from the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
 
3. SAP Business Process Test Automation (BPCA)
Part of SAP Solution Manager, analyzing the impact of changes on business processes.
Identifies transactions and objects potentially affected by an update.
Helps prioritize testing based on detected risks.
 
I hope this is answering your questions in detail.
 
Best regards,
Jeremy

entry.title: Re: CDS negative FLTP cast to decimal or floating in 7.5
entry.content: do you understand what max do?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 Public cloud - Service - Service quotation/re...
entry.content:
Hello,

The service quotation process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition follows these key steps:

  1. Create/change service quotation
  2. Send service quotation to customer
  3. Review service quotation (change/update if needed)
  4. Customer accepts or rejects service quotation
  5. If accepted, service order is automatically triggered
  6. If rejected, service quotation is closed

Some key points about service quotations:

  • They provide cost estimates to customers before placing a service order
  • Support both Time & Material (T&M) and Fixed Price estimates
  • Can be created manually or based on service requests
  • Allow negotiation and partial acceptance of items
  • Automatically create service orders upon acceptance
  • Can be sent to customers as printouts or PDF attachments via email 

Currently, the "copy from template" or "Use template" functionality is available for service orders but not for service quotations in the public cloud edition. This differs from the on-premise version where templates can be used for quotations.

The standard process recommended by SAP is to create the service quotation first, then convert it to a service order after customer acceptance. The quotation provides the initial cost estimate and scope, while the order manages the actual service execution.

For repair scenarios, SAP S/4HANA Cloud supports in-house repair processes, but a specific "Repair Quotation" button is not mentioned in the provided information. The repair process likely follows the standard service quotation to service order flow.

Regarding future enhancements, SAP continuously updates cloud solutions, but specific roadmap details for adding template functionality to service quotations are not provided in the given context.

To summarize, while templates are not currently available for quotations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition, the system still supports a comprehensive service quotation to order process. Consider providing feedback to SAP about the desired template functionality for potential future enhancements.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Advanced MPL using Elastic Stack
entry.content:
Hi Mikel, Hi Tim,

sorry for answering that late. I have not been notified by the platform for your messages.

Do I understand correctly that you try to send the payload to an on-premise stack via cloud connector?

I have not tried this, but I experimented with Cloud Connector and I know that you can achieve a lot (e.g. send SCI messages to your local machine)

For me it seems you correctly use the API. What you have to think about is:

- I am not aware of the proxyHost and proxyPort as request parameter. Have you tried to put the virtual host name and port you send to into logstashUrl?
- it might be necessary that you even set the host header, but try without first


Best Regards
Uwe

I want to retain the two most recent forecasts so that users can compare the current forecast with the previous one.

I tried using the Data Actions Advanced Editor but haven’t been successful.

How can I achieve this? Any guidance would be greatly appreciated!

1746429 - How to correct commitment inconsistencies

entry.title: SAP JAVA PO - Error "Alert Fatal: handshake failure" on webservice
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We get a “iaik.security.ssl.SSLException: Peer sent alert: Alert Fatal: handshake failure” error when testing a webservice:

The API doesn’t require User Authentication, It only requires a Header “apiKey” which was successfully entered.

We can get a successfull response on postman, seems the issue must be on the SAP PO system.

As per blogs and SAP note 2616423 this seems like a TLS error.

HTTPS is not enabled on the SAP PO system.  I see HTTPS is required for TLS 1.3.  Please confirm that the first step I should do is to enable HTTPS on the SAP PO system to point me in the right direction?  Or is HTTPS not relevant on the SAP PO system?

Kind Regards,

entry.title: SAP S/4 Out-of-the-Box Functionality in Plant Maintenance and EHS – Std. Features vs. Add-On Licens.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am currently exploring SAP S/4HANA and would like to understand the out-of-the-box (standard) functionalities available in the Plant Maintenance (PM) and Environment, Health & Safety (EHS) modules, without requiring any additional licensing or add-ons cost. 

Specifically, I would like to know:

  1. Work Clearance Management (WCM) & Permit to Work – Are these included as standard features in SAP S/4HANA, or do they require a separate license?
  2. Standard EHS Functionalities – What key capabilities are provided by default for incident management, risk assessment, and compliance tracking?
  3. SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) Features – Which functionalities are pre-configured in areas like work order management, preventive maintenance, and resource scheduling?
  4. Integration & Enhancements – How does SAP S/4HANA handle cross-module integration (e.g., EHS & PM) without additional costs, and what enhancements are available within the standard system

entry.content:
hello @everyone 

I want to translate the text in the acceptance criteria displayed in the mobile app. Can anyone help me?

thank you

entry.title: Re: Unable to close project / WBS
entry.content: Hello Ken, thank you so much for the answer. However, we are working with Public Cloud and we do not have the option to run the mentioned reports. Is there any alternative solution?

entry.title: Re: SAP JAVA PO - Error "Alert Fatal: handshake failure" on webservice
entry.content: The first thing to confirm is whether the necessary certificate chain from the PO system is trusted on the remote end, and vice versa for the certificates from the remote host in the PO system.

entry.title: Re: Creating substitiution exit
entry.content:
Forget my 'step' I shoud have written 'Call Point' (lost in translation)

BSEG fields (including ZZ fields) should be already available in substitutions. Look at table GB01 (view GB01C) or VWTYGB01 (SM30) there is an exclude flag to deactivate if required, in this case correct and execute RGUGBR00. Check also KBAs such as 3351552 - Usage of fields in Substitution and Validation in connection with GB01 transparent table (but for customer fields no problem)

conceptually use of field = 'constant' in the rule or create an exit routine where field = 'constant' does not change anything?

entry.title: The top 5 HR trends today – and HR's guide to what's next
entry.content:
Each year, the HR Research Scientists at SAP SuccessFactors conduct research to understand the top HR and workforce trends facing organizations and share our perspective on what HR teams should consider as they look to help their companies address these trends. This year we aggregated and synthesized data from 40 global and regional reputable business press sources that put forward 254 individual trends and predictions grounded in their own research and data. We then conducted a content analysis of the trends sample to derive the five key themes, or “meta-trends.” While our annual report always includes some pointed commentary and critique about each trend based on our expertise in psychology, new this year is calling upon our own body of original applied research to incorporate datapoints and insights, resulting in a more evidence-based point of view. 

This year’s trends are in different stages of maturity and on different trajectories; therefore, the role that HR needs to play to help businesses tackle and capitalize on these trends is different. We’ve organized the trends into two sections aligned to the dual role HR will play in addressing them.  

First, HR will need to act as a Conductor, leading the orchestration of a strategy and associated change management across the business to realize the opportunities these trends offer:  

Trend #1: Reconnecting the disconnected employee: Contentious decisions, macroeconomic and sociopolitical stressors, and breached trust with leadership has led to employee stress and burnout – and consequently, a crisis of disconnect and counterproductivity. In the year ahead: 

  • Leaders must ruthlessly prioritize fulfilling their end of the “psychological contract” by meeting employees’ basic needs. 
  • People managers will be seen as a lifeline for employees drowning in disconnect. 
  • STAT: 57% of employees feel unless their companies make some serious changes, their burnout will not get better. 

Trend #2: Moving from AI hype to AI impact:Organizations are shifting from AI pilot projects to enterprise-wide rollouts, demanding proof of clear value and ROI. In the year ahead: 

  • Organizations will home in on their key value drivers for AI, revealing their true priorities. 
  • The body of research on the ROI of AI will be built this year. 
  • Organizations will find friction between leaders’ and employees’ goals for using AI. 
  • STAT: 46% of employees feel that the time that they save by using AI tools at work belongs to them, not their organization.​ 

Trend #3: Striking a balance to steer skills forward: Organizations continue to face pervasive skills gaps, in part due to rapid AI advancements. A more balanced approach is needed to see tangible progress in skills-based transformations this year. In the year ahead: 

  • “Skills-based” will no longer be the only goal.  
  • Pay will prove itself the missing piece of the upskilling puzzle.  
  • The human vs. technical skill debate will move from or to and. 
  • STAT: 54% of employees would be more motivated to learn new skills if their company instituted skills-based pay.​ 

Second, HR will need to act as a Navigator, leading the organization through precarious waters and circumventing obstacles to put policies into practice for the betterment of all stakeholders:  

Trend #4: Divesting or doubling down on diversity, equity, inclusions, and belonging (DEI&B): Some organizations remain committed to DEI&B goals, continuing to ask “How are we going to do this?” Others plan to divest, instead now asking “Are we going to do this?” In the year ahead: 

  • Some will shy away from DEI&B goals, but these approaches will vary.   
  • Taking a stand on DEI&B will change company cultures in the long term, but it’s not clear exactly how. 
  • STAT: 26% of employees say companies focus too much on DEI&B, 41% of employees say companies focus an appropriate amount on DEI&B, and 33% of employees say companies focus too little on DEI&B. 

Trend #5: Plugging into or pulling the plug on hybrid work: Now that organizations have determined their position on where their employees will work, it’s time to see if they achieve the outcomes they intended. In the year ahead: 

  • Those businesses choosing the return-to-office path will see whether their bets paid off this year. 
  • Those choosing the hybrid or remote path will take it a step further, integrating autonomy as a core value in other aspects of work design. 
  • STAT: 54% of employees would consider being paid less if they could have more flexibility in where and when they work.  

Read the report to see what’s now and what’s next for each trend, along with some fast facts that uplevel the nerdiness of this year’s trends report. We also include a section on how SAP SuccessFactors solutions can help organizations address the 2025 HR trends.

entry.title: SAP UI5 mock sap/bc/ui2/start_up
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a complex freestyle UI5 application with different dependencies. In order to test the app I need many different types of data coming from varios sources. To save immense work and test case creation I want to mock my data. I can mock all my OData requests by using the 

sap-fe-mockserver in my ui5-local.yaml.
However I must mock /sap/bc/ui2/start_up because my app has a dependency of it as well. Unfortunately that's some sort of a service that's not OData and doesn't have proper metadata. I've tried to mock it with a 
customMiddleware in the yaml but UI5 can't resolve anything that should serve a custom JSON.
 
Can anyone suggest a way to mock a service / url request for GET requests ? I can do it via a custom node server or a python one with reverse proxy but I don't want to leave the SAP UI5 framework.

In a nutshell I want to be able to GET data from http://[domain]/custom/service
 in my case : http://localhost:8081/sap/bc/ui2/start_up

For example:

This one gives the following error:


 


Thanks in advance 🙂

entry.title: Re: Unable to close project / WBS
entry.content: If all the PRs and POs are final closed and there are no open commitments, please submit a trouble ticket to SAP support they will be able to fix it.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite – Connect Everything FEATURING WORKDAY
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog is the second part of our “Connect Everything” campaign. If you haven’t heard about it yet, check out the main blog post series for an overview of our campaign.

What does this blog cover?

In this post, we will demonstrate how easily a Workday solution can be integrated with SAP S/4HANA  Cloud. This seamless connection is made possible by the SAP Integration Suite, which ensures smooth and efficient integration between the two systems.

Integration Scenario

Many organizations use Workday as their primary HR system for managing employee data, while SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is used for enterprise operations. To ensure accurate HR and system access management, companies need a reliable integration that automatically replicates employee records from Workday to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, keeping workforce data consistent across systems.

With this integration, employees from Workday are created as Workforce Persons in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. At the same time, this process enables automatic user provisioning, meaning that once an employee is replicated, the corresponding business user is also created in SAP, granting them access to relevant applications and processes.

As shown in the process figure below, a scheduled job in SAP Cloud Integration queries Workday for any updated or newly created employee records. Workday processes the request and returns the relevant employee data. If no changes are found, the process ends. Otherwise, the data is transformed into the required SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition format. The transformed records are then sent to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, where employees are either created or updated as Workforce Persons, ensuring their details are up to date.

 

How to set up the scenario?

Overview:

  1. Find the right scenario
  2. Prepare SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  3. Prepare Workday
  4. Configure Cloud Integration

 

1.Find the right scenario at API Business Hub

SAP offers a wide range of pre-built integration content for Workday and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. To explore what’s available, visit the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, where you can filter by the systems you need and find ready-to-use integration packages that streamline the setup process.

  • Open SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  • Search for the package SAP S/4HANA Cloud Integration with Workday
  • Select Replicate Employee from Workday to SAP S4HANA Cloud

 

  • Here, you can discover and analyze the integration flow
  • Under Documents, you will find the link to the documentation, which you can read through


2.Prepare SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Create the Communication System and Communication User
    • Create new User for Inbound Communication
    • Save your credentials for cloud integration configuration

Communication System

Communication User

 

  • Create Communication Arrangement
  • Choose Scenario -> SAP_COM_0301
  • Maintain your created communication system and Inbound User 
  • Save the SOAP API Endpoint Replicate Basic Master Data for Workforce from External HR Systems for cloud integration configuration

Communication Arrangement

3.Prepare Workday

Check instruction in the documentation of the Integration Flow -> Configuration Guide

4.Configure Cloud Integration

  • As a prerequisite, first install the Workday Adapter from the discover menu for prepackaged integration content  in your environment
  • Once successfully copied to your design runtime, deploy the Workday Adapter.
  • After you install the Workday Adapter, copy the package ->SAP S/4HANA Cloud Integration with Workday to your own design runtime

Workday Package

  • Select the value mapping artifact
  • Maintain your value mapping for the GenderCode and CompanyCode, as required in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Select the Replicate Employee from Workday to SAP S4HANA Cloud  Integration Flow and click on configure

Artifacts

  • Here maintain your Workday tenant configuration values like tenant url, credentials 
  • Maintain the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition parameters, which were set up in the previous step.
    • Configure the inbound endpoint of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 
    • Maintain also SAP S/4HANA Cloud credentials for authentication.

Conclusion 

Setting up the Workday to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Integration was easier than expected, thanks to prebuilt content available in the SAP Integration Suite. Instead of starting from scratch, we could leverage ready-to-use integration flows that only required minimal configuration. However, for businesses with more complex needs, there’s always the option to customize or build entirely new integrations

Have you worked on a Workday-SAP integration before? What were your key takeaways? Let’s start a discussion—share your insights or drop your questions in the comments. And stay tuned for more content in our "Connect Everything" series!

entry.title: Re: Ppm item closing is resulting in error
entry.content: Please check with SAP Support to get some spot consulting to resolve your issue, they will need to work in your system.

entry.title: CPROJECTS
entry.content:

How can I know what fiori app has a service implemented to explore archived documents?

Thanks and regards

David Sánchez

entry.title: Re: Flow Builder: Using exit to fetch HBKID/HKTID for Accounting Documents missed those information
entry.content:
Hello @Hongjun_Qian ,

First of all I want to say: excellent blog. Very useful.

 

I have the component SAP S/4HANA 2022 

I just want to share with you my issue when try the re-generation the loading class for Flow Builder. The selection-screen do not show the block with FI Operations.

When I execute the t-code FCLM_FB_UTIL I got this screen:

I appreciate your help.

Thanks in advance.

Kr,

Renato

entry.title: Re: Data Migration Cockpit: TRCO transformation rule fram...
entry.content:
Hello Moritz,

which S/4HANA release are you working with? It is hard to tell from only one screenshot, but it seems, that some mapping values, which you need to process the instances (maybe to simulate or migrate) have not been confirmed yet. If you want to run a simulation at least all the mapping values, which are relevant for the instances you want to simulate/migrate need to be confirmed in the mapping task screen.

The error message you got seems to be from an older version of S/4HANA. In the meantime the error messages have been adapted to guide the user to the mapping task.

I hope this helps to resolve your issue.

Best regards, Kirstin

entry.title: Re: translate the text in the acceptance Criteria
entry.content:
Hello Aziz,

 

The mentioned text in Acceptance Criteria is a Value. [which can be modified as required]

Please refer https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_admin/value-translations.html#supported-object-types-for-value-translation

Only above objects are supported for Value Translations

Hence, the mentioned Text cannot be translated

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Unable to close project / WBS

entry.title: Facing issue with same material number ciffing from ECC and S4 system to APO system
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

We have two source systems (ECC and S4)  connected to APO system.

"We are trying to transfer material master from S4 System To APO system which is having same material number in ECC system as well, but it is not transferring to APO from S4 systema and we are getting CIF error "Product AA111 was created in a different business system group BSG1". Since we have correct BSG setup in APO system like BSG1 for ECC and BSG2 for S4 system.

Kindly suggest  for this issue to transfer material from S4 To APO which is having same number in ECC system.

entry.title: SAML and 2212795
entry.content:
Hello !

I configured a BO system (4.3 SP4 PL7) to enable SAML SSO and it is working fine.

But as soon as I configure the tomcat to redirect http requests to https as per note 2212795, the SAML logon gets ignored.

Anybody here already tried this and can give me a hint, what possibly goes wrong?

YOS,

Markus A. Engelkamp

entry.title: Re: Dynamically Increase/Decrease Background/Dialog Work Processes in SAP
entry.content:
Excellent information and Small correction / may be the typo mistake in the parameter. ( rdips/wp_max_no )

The parameter rdisp/wp_max_no defines the maximum number of work processes that the server can start and therefore defines the size of the internal process table.

During the start-up of the server, the work processes resulting from the following parameters are started:
rdisp/wp_no_dia
rdisp/wp_no_vb
rdisp/wp_no_vb2
rdisp/wp_no_enq
rdisp/wp_no_btc
rdisp/wp_no_spo
If the operating mode is switched, the number of individual process types can be changed.
The criterion that the total must not exceed the value of rdisp/max_wp_no still applies.
The "new" possibility of creating processes dynamically is also used for handling deadlocks.

entry.title: App to App Seamless Logon
entry.content:
I have multiple Native Android MDK Applications to deploy on rugged devices in a warehouse environment.  I would like to have my users only log into a single Application and then when calling another application, the logon is seamless.  My BTP Destination is using Principle Propagation.  

I have read that with Principle Propagation the Token is not exposed, so Token Exchange is not an option.  Is there documentation on how to pass authentication between applications?

entry.title: How to get the mapped fieldnames from a result value in an action method?
entry.content:
Hi,

I try to read the actual entity with the following EML:

READ ENTITIES OF zrap_a_sales IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY zsaleshd
      ALL FIELDS WITH
     CORRESPONDING #( keys )
     RESULT DATA(lt_zsales).

The signature of the method:

checkdataset FOR MODIFY
     IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION zsaleshd~checkdataset RESULT result.

I tried already all combinations of "MAPPING FROM ENTITY" but nothing works until yet.

Thank's for any hints!

Side Note, we have 2 Competency section with different rating scales in PM.

However I still wonder how it worked with Default rating scale last year. Something interesting to investigate from our side 😉 

Thanks in advance!

Regards,

Mano

entry.title: Proper exception handling for CX_UUID_ERROR?
entry.content:
I have a database table with a field GUID as primary key (TYPE RAW16). Now, I want to insert a row, and therefore I use cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ) in order to create the key.

I am looking for the best approach to deal with the exception CX_UUID_ERROR. My current thought is not to handle it at all! So, if the creation of the GUID failed, a dump would occur. Not nice, but what is a better alternative?

Of course, I could catch the exception, but I have no proper idea what to do in the handler. Setting an initial value for the GUID before the INSERT e.g. doesn't seem to be a good solution either...

Any recommendations?

By the way, I am wondering why SAP has inherited from CX_STATIC_CHECK instead of CX_NO_CHECK...

data: ls_row type zmy_table.

* Omit exception handling on purpose...
ls_row-guid = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ). 
ls_row-field1 = 'any content'.
insert zmy_table from ls_row.
...

entry.title: Re: Executing simple REST requests with SAP CAP applications
entry.content:
Hi Tobias,

great easy example.

What is necessary to make this run, when deployed to BTP into a CF environment?
Locally it's totally fine but not deployed.

KR,
Ruben

entry.title: Re: Different Papersize between CR Designer and RAS
entry.content:
It could be that the Business Objects servers don't have the required printer drivers installed on them so it doesn't have the proper files to communicate with your printer.

We've seen differences with printer driver files between a local developers machine and our servers.  Our server admins don't want to install printer drivers on our servers so we tell our report developers to check the "No Printer" option.  This allows the report format to be rendered as it would on the screen.

Of course, we don't have anything that is actually printed from our servers so this helps our configuration.

entry.title: Re: How to publish Business Objects reports to SharePoint?
entry.content: There was an add-on that was required to have integration with SharePoint.  I'm not sure if it is still available.  You would need to look through the software downloads on the support site to see if it is still available for BI4.3.

entry.title: Re: Different Papersize between CR Designer and RAS
entry.content:
Hi, another thing I found out.

When I edit with CR 2020 the label report, I adjust printer name and paper size, the report is correctly printed in the ERP.

But when I change the page setup setting within my tool program (like Don's tool) to the same values, the report becomes incorrect.

So there are some differences between the CRD "Page setup" logic and RAS, especially with:
-> PrintOutputController.ModifyPrintOptions

f.ex. when the report is saved with CRD, the Printer-Duplex property is
CrystalDecisions.ReportAppServer.ReportDefModel.CrPrinterDuplexEnum.crPrinterDuplexDefault

Once changed another prop in my tool with ModifyPrintOptions (not the PrinterDuplex-prop), PrinterDuplex becomes automatically crPrinterDuplexSimplex after saving the report (with reportClientDoc.SaveAs)?? How can this happen?

Thanks in advance,
Patrick

entry.title: Dynamic Interface on Cloud Integration - part 4
entry.content:
I’ll break this article into:

  • Problem/Context 
  • Our approach 
  • Git into Partner Directory 
  • Generic iflow (this link)
  • Automated unit testing
  • Limitations and Future plans

As with any interface, this generic iflow is split in three parts:

  1. The receiver protocol
  2. Transformations
  3. The sender

Receiver

To receive the data from our consumers we support AS2, HTTP, AMQP adapter, AdvancedEventMesh adapter. I'll not show here all details but lets go through some of the most interesting parts.

If you remember from part 2, the url for our scenario was:

https://<cloudintegrationrturl>/http/TestpackageNUPE/FER_DynamicInterfaceTransformAndRoute/NUPEBPAPOC

By configuring our receive as above (with the * wildcard), we allow any interfaceid to come as part of the request. How do intercept which interface id have we received? Using a content modifier as below

Transformations

With this key id, we have everything we need not to check which configuration exists for this on partner directory. This can be achieved by querying the Partner Directory API as done below:

 

 

def service = ITApiFactory.getApi(PartnerDirectoryService. class , null);
    if (service == null) {
        throw new IllegalStateException("Partner Directory Service not found");
    }
    def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message);
    if (messageLog != null) {
        messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty("FER_InterfaceID", interfaceId);
    }
    try {
        def lookupRules = service.getParameter("LookupRules", namespace + interfaceId, String. class);
        if (lookupRules != null) {
            def decodedBytes = Base64.getDecoder().decode(lookupRules)
            String lookupRulesText = new String(decodedBytes, "UTF-8");
            logMessage <<= "Found lookup rules [${lookupRulesText}] for partner id [${namespace+interfaceId}]\n";

 

 

Then we parse the lookupRules file loaded and we iterate all rules there one by one. I'll save you from those details, it's just groovy code. 

After complete iteration we'll have the final parameter set and values and the payload was transformed already according to the respective rules.

If something goes wrong (an exception is thrown), or if we want to see the details of all lookups and transformations and intermediate payloads we can enable debug or trace into the iflow. In any of these cases we'll then dump a TraceLog file into the MPL as attachment so that it can be checked later on by our support team.

 

 

String logLevel = message.getProperty('SAP_MessageProcessingLogConfiguration').logLevel.toString()
        if (logLevel in ['DEBUG', 'TRACE'] || (traceLog!=null && !"".equals(traceLog))) {
            logMessageAsAttachment = true;
        }
    } catch (Exception ex) {
        logMessageAsAttachment = true;
        throw ex;
    } finally {
        if (logMessageAsAttachment && isLoggingOnErrorEnabled) {
            String logMessageStr = logMessage.toString()
            message.setProperty("log", logMessageStr)
            messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)?.addAttachmentAsString('TraceLog', logMessageStr, 'text/plain')
        }
    }

 

 

Sender

Now we can optionally have a process direct step (depending on the configuration files defined) pointing into a custom iflow

This can be very handy whenever the target requires a non-standard delivery approach (fetching special tokens or nounces, etc). If that's the case we can still leverage all transformations done before and use the process direct just to develop the delivery part.

If the target is quite standard, it will likely fall into something our generic iflow already supports. For sending the data we support the following adapters: HTTP, IDOC, AdvancedEventMesh, AMQP and SFTP. Others can be added later if needed but for now, we had no need to enhance it further.

In this chapter we covered everything from "runtime" perspective, meaning picking the configurations from partner directory and executing according to the payload that was supplied. On next chapter we'll talk about unit testing

As usual, feel free to provide ideas or suggestions based on your experience

entry.title: Re: CDS negative FLTP cast to decimal or floating in 7.5
entry.content: are you sure? then tell me why it is max's fault?

entry.title: SAP IBP Configuration - Configure Last Period Aggregation in SAP IBP
entry.content:
Last Period Aggregation (LPA) in SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) is a method used to aggregate key figures by taking the value from the last period within the aggregation level. This is particularly useful when you need to ensure that the most recent data point is used for planning and analysis.

For example, if you are aggregating weekly data into monthly data, LPA will take the value from the last week of the month rather than summing or averaging the weekly values.

This approach is often used for key figures like projected stock, where the end-of-period value is more relevant than intermediate values.

In this article we are going to configure the static aggregation using the IBP_LPA function. In the example, to get the value of Inventory Target Quantity for the end of a quarter, we will use the formula to compute the value of Aggregated Inventory Target at the end of the last month of the quarter.

Create Planning Level

1. Create a planning level QRTPRODLOC by copying planning level PERPRODLOC. Ensure that Quarterly is the root. Use the following data:

FieldValueRoot
NameQRTPRODLOC 
DescriptionQuarter / Product / Location 
Time Attributes:  
 QuarterlyX
 Yearly 
Attributes:  
I49PRODUCT  
 PRDIDX
 PRDDESCR 
 PRDFAMILY 
 ABCCODE 
I49LOCATION  
 LOCIDX
 LOCDESCR 

Select lowest time profile level Quarterly

Enter ID and Description

Add attributes

2. Create a key figure called AGGREGATEDINVENTORY at the QRTPRODLOC planning level, which will display inventory at the end of the quarter.
Use the following data.

FieldValue
Key Figure IDAGGREGATEDINVENTORY
Base Planning LevelQRTPRODLOC
NameAggregated Inventory
CalculatedX

Enter Key Figure ID and other information

3. Add the following calculation: AGGREGATEDINVENTORY@QRTPRODLOC = IBP_LPA(“INVENTORYTARGETQTY @MTHPRODLOC”)

Add Calculation Definition, enter the calculation AGGREGATEDINVENTORY@QRTPRODLOC = IBP_LPA(“INVENTORYTARGETQTY @MTHPRODLOC”)

Select INVENTORYTARGETQTY@MTHPRODLOC as stored.

Check the request level calculation and ensure that AGGREGATEDINVENTORY@QRTPRODLOC is calculated.

4. Activate planning area.

Test Solution Using Planner Workspace

1. Test solution by creating a Planner Workspace using the following information.

FieldValue
Time 
Time PeriodQuarterly
FromCurrent Quarter
ToCurrent Quarter + 4
RollingYes
Planning Level 
Product IDSelected
Location IDSelected
Key Figures 
Aggregated InventorySelected
Inventory Target QtySelected
Filters 
Product IDx100 Phone
Location ID101

The value of Inventory target qty is 100 in the last month of the first quarter. 

the value of Aggregated inventory qty is 100 in the first quarter, equal to inventory target qty of last month of first quarter.

entry.title: Re: How can we integrate SAP C4C Sales Order with SAP Mar...
entry.content:
Hey @ShoaibPatel,

If you're using SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) V1, the typical approach to integrating Sales Order data with SAP Marketing Cloud (SMC) would be via BTP (CPI) using standard API/OData services.
However, since SMC is being sunset, what’s your plan for transitioning away from SMC and using SAP Emarsys or another marketing tool?

If it helped, mark thread as accepted solution.

Thanks & Regards,
Kunal Bansal, SAP CX SME
https://www.linkedin.com/in/thekunalbansal

entry.title: hyperinflation Argentin material Management revaluation process
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are implementing the SAP standard hyperinflation module in Argentina for the client EPTA and require specific support regarding the material revaluation process within Material Management (MM).

Current Challenges Identified:

  • The revaluation process impacts multiple accounts, but Material Ledger posts the adjustment to a single account.
  • The system allows for revaluation using market price, inflation index, or both, but does not differentiate hyperinflation effects from other price variances.
  • Material revaluation impacts accounting but does not generate material documents, which affects traceability.
  • Stock and COGS valuation adjustments do not separate hyperinflation effects from other cost variances within the standard functionalities.

Key Points to Address:

  1. Material Revaluation Process:
    • How can the material revaluation be configured to ensure hyperinflation adjustments are separately accounted for within Material Ledger?
    • Are there standard options to post revaluation adjustments to multiple accounts instead of consolidating them into one?
    • How can SAP ensure that material stock valuation correctly reflects the impact of hyperinflation without distorting other price variances?
  3. Configuration and System Behavior:
    • What is the recommended configuration in MM and Material Ledger to properly handle inflation adjustments?
    • How can we ensure that the revaluation process updates both stock value and material cost transparently in SAP reports?
  5. Standard Capabilities vs. Customization Needs:
    • Does SAP offer a standard way to separate hyperinflation effects from other price variances within material revaluation?
    • If standard functionalities do not allow this, what are SAP"s recommended best practices to achieve this separation without custom developments?

We would appreciate the opportunity to discuss these points with an SAP specialist. Please let us know your availability to schedule a meeting.

  • Their IT account
  • They have cleared cache and cookies

Does anyone know what else I could try?

 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: I am looking for specific fields to use in Approval d...
entry.content:
Hello,

I am assuming you are referring about the self-services tactical sourcing RFQ request and once it is awarded , we convert the same to create a requisition and you are looking for reference of RFQ details in requisition to control certain approval node behaviour. 

We have a "GBRequestRef" OOTB field available which host the value of RFQ in case the requisition is originated from RFQ Form. The field is available in Requisition Header section. 

Please explore the "GBRequestRef" in your approval node. Also please note the value of the field would be null in case of normal requisition so please ensure you handle null value properly in your approval node condition. Attached the screenshot of same for your reference. 

Hope this help!

Regards,

Ajay Kumar

entry.title: How to use the Libraries effectively in SAP Cloud ALM?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Cloud ALM Community,

I'm a big fan of using things properly. I was very curious when I saw in today's release notes a mention about libraries, and the possibility of assigning test cases to libraries.

  1. I was a bit disappointed I had never heard about such thing as "Libraries", even if I went through all the available SAP Cloud ALM trainings.
  2. The next point which is a bit strange for me, I can't find the Libraries app anywhere when I look into the Implementations section, or Operations, or whatever. I can only find it, when I specifically search for it on the search bar. Why?

Now, please help me. I went through the available documentation on Libraries - Library Management | SAP Help Portal

I have no idea how to assign test cases to a Library element. And I have no idea if this Library element could be reused among projects. This would be a great way to reuse test cases, as the concept of "Library" is typically to have something which can be reused to improve efficiency (like dlls?).

Thanks so much.

entry.title: Create a new entry in view: Update control/ WM movement types in transac. OMJJ?
entry.content:
Hi gurus.

How can I create a new entry in view: Update control/ WM movement types in transac. OMJJ?

I need a entry with parameters 101_XQB_V

This is error:

Update control of movement type is incorrect (entry 101 _ X Q B _ V)

Message no. M7226

Diagnosis

The system could not find entry 101 _ X Q B _ V in any of the movement type control tables.

Procedure

Cancel current processing.

Contact your system administrator.

Action to be taken by the system administrator

Check in Customizing for Inventory Management under Copy, Change Movement Types whether the entry is contained in the Update control view for the movement type.
If the entry is missing, proceed as follows:

  • For standard movement types, this means that the given process is not supported using the present data combination. Do not create any entries without prior arrangement with your SAP consultant. Otherwise, incorrect postings may result.
  • For customer-defined movement types (beginning with 9, X, Y, or Z), this may mean that they have not been fully defined. Delete the movement type and re-create it by copying a standard movement type.

 

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Security audit log
entry.content:
This issue is further explained and resolved by using the SAP Note : 3495710.

 

Error Caused: This appears to be caused by a mismatch in some security Audit Log parameters and is revealing itself now with the upgrade to higher patch level of kernel 793.

Which enforces a more restrictive check of these parameters.

 

Impact : a lot of syslog messages will be written, and the syslog messages will be overwritten and same can be seen in transaction SM21

 

Solution :

FIN_AUDIT : if both profile parameters are set to 0 then the mode of creating “one Audit File per Day” is active.

If the parameter currently contains the date placeholder characters “++++++++ then it would need to have the file number placeholder characters appended at the end to become “audit ++++++++_######” instead.

See also SAP Note : 3495710.

entry.title: Re: How to disable the Editing Status Filter in RAP Fiori...

You can find the answer in the following link: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/8eb695ac473b4ab0a726e4021ce35cf4?q=editing%20status

entry.title: Re: SHDB transaction is not checking S_BDC_MONI
entry.content: After i compare the authorization trace, the system (have object restriction) is showing the object S_BDC_MONI but the other system (dont check object restriction) does not show the object.

entry.title: Re: Dyanmic Breaks and Gaps in Working Time
entry.content:
Hello Manon,

I believe this is expected system behavior. In the first example you provided, the employee is working from 8 until 1pm, thus 5 hours, and then, since there are 15 mins are unaccounted for (1-1:15)before the break can be applied, even if he works from 1:15  to 1:45 so he would have the extra 30 mins required to get the break, he's only getting 15 mins break instead of 30 mins (because the unrecorded 15 mins are deducted). In the second example however, when he has worked from 8 until 1:30pm he has already completed 5 hours and a half (uninterrupted), so he becomes eligible for a 30min break between 1:30 and 2, and the system is disregarding that after this threshold there are those 15 mins unaccounted for (between 2 and 2:15). From what I've checked, the validation related to the gaps in working times is applied before the break, not after.

entry.title: Re: SHDB transaction is not checking S_BDC_MONI
entry.content: i check the parameter "bdc/bdel_auth_check" but it looks kinda same. As for the "bdc/shdb/auth_check" parameter is not exist in my system.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to display Custom Application Logs in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hi @Daniii I suggest for you to read the full section of SAP Help related to Application Logs, as well as implementing the code snippets provided there:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/read-application-logs-from-database 

Once you make them work for your scenario. Then you need to implement a Custom Entity with a Class that reads the logs using the logic you created in the step above. I suggest for you to follow next tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-a4c-create-custom-entity.html There are plenty helpful tutorials about Custom Entities, in case you need additional information.

I hope this helps.

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP – SAP Customer Engagement Initiative 2025-1 for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a complete, modular Cloud ERP software designed for every business need – powered by AI and analytics. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can run your mission-critical operations in real time from anywhere, introduce new business models in your industry, and expand globally with a trusted partner.  

An exciting opportunity to influence SAP has begun with the first cycle in 2025. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner, to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions. 

The projects will be open for customer registration until  March 28, 2025. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud projects for registration: 

  • Bank Relationship Management: Improving Overview, Services & Fee, and Interest Calculations
  • Fiori First for Sales Order Management
  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Insights into Business processes with Business Event Logging
  • Joule and GenAI use cases in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • New Financial Reporting for Enterprise Project (Project Booklet) including ETO
  • Planned New Solution for Cost Component Breakdown
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies across different roles in a project
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Product Change Management
  • Simplification of transports in SAP S/4HANA® Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape
  • Usability Improvements for managing attachments in S/4HANA business applications

Seize this opportunity to make your voices heard – register on influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025! If you are also interested in other SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects that are currently open for registration, check out the full list here. 

Warm regards, 

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Re: Go for your quick win! Migrate Identity Provisioning tenants to SAP Cloud Identity infrastructur
entry.content:
Hi Ivelina,

when starting the migration IPS neo to CF we got a 401 error:
Job threw an unhandled exception. [See nested exception: Request failed with status: 401 and body: {"origin":"SAP IPS","error":{"code":"Unauthorized","message":""}}] Caused by: Job threw an unhandled exception. Caused by: Request failed with status: 401 and body: {"origin":"SAP IPS","error":{"code":"Unauthorized","message":""}}

We cannot find any additional Information anywere in IPS Neo or CIS CF. All users seem fine. When trying to migrate again when selecting the the "Identity Authentication tenant" we get "Internal error. Contact your system administrator." Again no additional information can be found. Do you have an idea what the issue might be?

entry.title: Re: Different Papersize between CR Designer and RAS
entry.content: OK. I only work with Business Objects installed on servers so that was the perspective that I answered from. I don't know much about using the RAS in a stand alone option.

entry.title: Re: Error when trying to install ADT in Eclipse
entry.content:
It is difficult to determine the exact issue. Based on the provided data, it appears to be a network problem (possibly some form of restricted access).
Perhaps the network administrator of your office network can provide additional information.

If you continue to experience issues, please open a ticket (component 'BC-DWB-AIE') and attach the 'Error Log' from Eclipse. It can be exported from the UI ('Error Log' View).

entry.title: Re: Dyanmic Breaks and Gaps in Working Time
entry.content:
Hi Iulia,

Thanks for the feedback. Do you have any suggestions on how I can stop this from happening or workaround this? To ensure that the 15 minutes is counted towards the break?

Thanks,

Manon Holman
